programme.title: A Place in the Sun
programme.desc: Danni Menzies helps Poppy and her dad Philip realise their dream of finding a holiday home in Andalucía for their budget of £50k, that all the family can use to enjoy the Spanish sunshine.

programme.title: Teleshopping
programme.desc: Shopping from home.

programme.title: Teleshopping
programme.desc: Shopping from home.

programme.title: Location, Location, Location
programme.desc: Kirstie and Phil are in North London helping two sets of househunters with very different problems. Ify Okoye is searching for her first home, having already viewed 90 properties has househunting fatigue. Phils with first time buyers Mike and Rosemary, who have spent the last six years abroad and want to settle down. Will their property aspirations prove too big for their budget?

programme.title: Stacey Solomons Renovation Rescue
programme.desc: In Cambridgeshire, Staceys with Shariqa and Imani, whove struggled to keep their renovation project on schedule or on budget. Can Stacey help them finally fly the nest?

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: Its the third night in Bedfordshire and Cambridgeshire and the turn of marketing executive Martina to cook. She hopes her menu of Thai curries and poached pears will impress her guests, one of whom doesnt like spice or fruit.

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The BandBers head to Simon and Vanessas coastal guesthouse in Weymouth, where delightful decor, sumptuous sausages and proper milk await them.

programme.title: A New Life in the Sun
programme.desc: In Italy, theres a tight turnaround for Angie and Ash. In Portugal, Harriet and Mondo branch out into events. And in Spain, Rachael and Lee bid to earn a crust at a pie shop.

programme.title: A Place in the Sun
programme.desc: Claire and Simon are starting a new adventure in Nerja in Spain. Will any of Craig Rowes options for their £230k get their hearts racing enough to put in an offer?

programme.title: A Place in the Sun: What Happened Next
programme.desc: On Cyprus, is Uri and Hazels vivacious villa still delivering the dream? And we see what Karmen has done to her meant-to-be Mijas Pueblo bolthole.

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: First to host in Lichfield, Staffs is business consultant James Herbert, who has high hopes for his complex Italian menu. He also has some fun photo frolics planned. His guests are 22-year-old football coach Tim Travers, 30-year-old sales assistant and all-round party boy JP Goodyear-Martinez, 38-year-old businesswoman Amanda Smith, and straight-talking retired mental health nurse Ronnie Imlah.

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: Ronnie Imlah hosts in Lichfield, with a menu inspired by dishes eaten at friends parties. With a revelation about Ronnies past leaving guests in shock, JP Goodyear-Martinez and Amanda Smith argue over plastic surgery.

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: JP Goodyear-Martinez hosts in Lichfield, treating his guests to a mix of Jamaican food, tight lycra outfits and a drag queen. James Herberts rendition of `Bohemian Rhapsody doesnt go down too well with Ronnie.

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: Football coach Tim Travers is determined to prove his kitchen skills despite never having cooked before. Luckily, his mum is on the other end of the phone to help out every step of the way with his American-themed menu.

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: Its the final night in Lichfield and there are high hopes for recruitment consultant Amanda Smiths evening, with her guests expecting a well-executed and sophisticated menu. An accident at the table has the guests in hysterics and straight-talking Ronnie describing it as her worst nightmare.

programme.title: A Place in the Sun
programme.desc: Frances dreams of buying a holiday home that she can enjoy with her friends and family in Spains beautiful coastal town of Nerja. Danni Menzies helps her find the perfect place for her budget of £200k.

programme.title: George Clarkes Old House, New Home
programme.desc: In Stroud, Phil and Leighla transform their Georgian farmhouse with an industrial-style extension, and in Wootton Bassett, a couple flips the layout of their Victorian end-of-terrace home.

programme.title: The Race for Ancient Egypt in Colour
programme.desc: As Egypt pushes for greater authority over its ancient heritage, foreign archaeologists rush to uncover the next Tutankhamun. But as the world goes to war, the race for ancient Egypt is paused.

programme.title: Roman Empire by Train with Alice Roberts
programme.desc: On the final leg of her journey, Alice heads south to Tarragona, once the capital of Iberian Rome. She learns how the Romans conquered Spain and gained control of the entire Mediterranean.

programme.title: Bill Baileys Vietnam
programme.desc: Bill Bailey heads to Hanoi, to uncover the countrys complex relationship with life, death, and war. On the shores of the Red Delta, Bill discovers a culture defined by spirituality, femininity and a unique method of honouring those who have passed.

programme.title: The Hunt: Prey vs Predator
programme.desc: Deep in a vast, remote forest, 10 players enter an extraordinary contest of stamina and strategy, all competing to win up to £100,000. The players are pitted against each other as Prey versus Predator, and will battle to survive action-packed hunts.

programme.title: The Hunt: Prey vs Predator
programme.desc: The adventure contest continues as the players discover who is the first to be culled. The group barely have time to recover from this shocking revelation before the hunt begins again. Will alliances count for anything in the thrill of the chase?

programme.title: The Hunt: Prey vs Predator
programme.desc: Eight contestants remain - and one decides its time to abandon their role as underdog. As fortunes flip in the forest, a dramatic twist turns the game on its head, putting even the strongest players in peril ahead of a tense and unexpected climax.

programme.title: Traffic Cops
programme.desc: Action-packed documentary series following Yorkshire-based law enforcers.

programme.title: Police Interceptors
programme.desc: The chopper goes after an armed robber, Zippy the sniffer dog makes an interesting discovery and the teams new recruits are put to the test by a pair of suspected burglars.

programme.title: Police Interceptors
programme.desc: The team pulls over a very angry driver, a car thief leads the officers on a cross-county chase, and the Air Support Unit has its work cut out pursuing an athletic burglar.

programme.title: Run for Cover
programme.desc: Matt Dow is a wanderer accused of a crime he didnt commit. A shoot-up takes place in which his young companions leg is smashed. When the townspeople realise they have made a terrible mistake, they appoint Dow their sheriff, and he in turn appoints his young crippled friend Davey his deputy. Daveys bitterness pulls the two apart, however, when he makes a deal with a gang of crooks.

programme.desc: A daily round-up of showbiz news and gossip from around the world, focusing on celebrities, movies, music and entertainment.

programme.title: A Man Alone
programme.desc: Wes Steele is a gunfighter on the run for his life for crimes he didnt commit. Escaping a lynch mob, he stumbles onto a small Arizona town where the local sheriff Gill Corrigan and his daughter Nadine are quarantined for yellow fever.

programme.title: Trucking Heavy
programme.desc: Sam must carefully lift a shipping container thats been converted into a summer cabin over a high hedge and through a narrow gap barely wider than the container itself! (S3 Ep18)

programme.title: Traffic Cops
programme.desc: The team chase a gang using 4x4s on nightly thefts from industrial estates. In Leeds, a teen crosses a busy road, and soon needs the full range of emergency services to save his life. In Bradford, the cops get reports of a child hit by a car.

programme.title: Police Interceptors
programme.desc: Profiling the work of high-speed police interception units around the country with some of the most dangerous pursuits and shocking footage ever captured. Exploring what it takes to bring order to chaos.

programme.title: Police Interceptors
programme.desc: When a dog handler is taken out of the chase, multiple cops and the police chopper take up the hunt for a driver who flees on foot.

programme.title: Motorway Cops: Catching Britains Speeders
programme.desc: A cop pursues a vehicle after a report of a cloned car with a balaclava-wearing driver. In Byker, PCs witness what looks like a drug deal through a car window.

programme.title: Traffic Cops: Pursuit Squad One
programme.desc: Near Harrogate, cop Paddy chases a driver seen using his phone, leading to a dangerous high-speed pursuit. On the A1, cop Paul races to intercept a suspected fraudster.

programme.title: Police: Night Shift 999
programme.desc: We start with reports of a break-in at a garage. Officers receive a call about a fight with a knife and discover a trail of blood leading into a block of flats.

programme.title: The X-Files
programme.desc: When death row inmate Luther Lee Boggs claims to be psychic and able to lead Mulder to a serial killer in return for a lesser sentence of life in prison, Scully becomes an unwilling believer.

programme.title: Mud Mountain Haulers
programme.desc: Craig and the crew are back in camp to log Saddle Ridge, but the crew discover the block is chock-full of boulders, making it impossible to log. One of the Glovers winch machines spews thick white smoke. They receive bad news from the government.

programme.title: Outback Truckers
programme.desc: Steve Graham is racing the rain and joining forces to build a mega road train. Meanwhile, on a cross-country marathon, Yogis truck hits the wall.

programme.title: Trucking Hell
programme.desc: Super-sized transport jobs require even bigger trucks to perform them. Lifting the lid on the heavy recovery business, featuring industrial strength trucks capable of towing and lifting the biggest and heaviest machines on the nations roads.

programme.title: Winter Road Rescue
programme.desc: Snowplough drivers and patrols are prepared to work all day and all night to keep UK roadways clear from obstruction when winter sweeps the nation. Fearless in the face of a cold front, these men and women are there to assist when disaster strikes.

programme.title: Police Interceptors
programme.desc: A drunk driver leads the interceptors on a merry-go-round. The team joins forces with the chopper to track down a wanted criminal on a motorbike, and a young driver crashes straight into an unmarked police car.

programme.title: Police Interceptors
programme.desc: PC Ange Salous confronts a not-so-happy chappy, and a torrential downpour hampers police at a major crash scene on the M1.

programme.title: In Old California
programme.desc: Tom Craig is a mild-mannered druggist from Boston who opens a shop in wild and woolly Sacramento shortly before the Gold Rush. The town is `owned by the Dawson brothers, Britt and Joe, who poison Craigs tonic when saloon hostess Lacey Miller takes too much of an interest in the handsome newcomer. Town drunk Whitey has one drink too many, and all of Sacramento is soon in a lynching mood.

programme.title: My Name Is Nobody
programme.desc: A gunfighter heading for New Orleans to board a ship and retire peacefully to Europe is prevented from hanging up his pistols by the persistent admiration of a young gunman called Nobody. Initially hired to kill the veteran fighter, Nobody becomes his friend and insists that the great mans career should end with a glorious climax.

programme.title: Trucking Heavy
programme.desc: A vintage 1950s Class 08 diesel electric shunter needs shifting cross-country but a traffic snarl up and a couple of tricky roundabouts could make this a challenging delivery.

programme.title: Traffic Cops
programme.desc: North Yorkshire is considered a soft touch by criminals using the countrys back roads to come in and out under cover of darkness. The traffic cops hunt down burglars, drug dealers, petrol bikers and thieves.

programme.title: Police Interceptors
programme.desc: Profiling the work of high-speed police interception units around the country with some of the most dangerous pursuits and shocking footage ever captured. Exploring what it takes to bring order to chaos.

programme.title: Police Interceptors
programme.desc: A dangerous driver whos breached his restraining order leads the interceptors on the pursuit to end all pursuits: a half-hour, white-knuckle ride across Nottinghamshire.

programme.title: Motorway Cops: Catching Britains Speeders
programme.desc: Police tackle a drink driver at 8am, an uninsured pizza delivery man, and a reckless driver wanting a selfie despite having no licence or insurance.

programme.title: Trucking Hell
programme.desc: When a fully loaded HGV rolls over on the A6, Fluff and his team need to create a second mess to solve the first. Jim is sent to recover a broken down truck from a waste tip facility, but working among the rubbish is not particularly pleasant.

programme.title: The X-Files
programme.desc: A series of sexually oriented murders that are identical, except that the killer appears to be both male and female, draws Scully and Mulder to an Amish-type community of people who may be alien in origin.

programme.title: Mud Mountain Haulers
programme.sub-title: Theres No Guarantee, but Theres Skill
programme.desc: With road restrictions and spring break-up on the horizon, $1 million worth of timber could be stranded unless Craig figures out a plan. Brent is behind on logging and clearing a campsite and avoids catastrophe while uprooting a giant tree.

programme.title: Outback Truckers
programme.desc: In Townsville, Queensland, long-distance trucker Peter is getting set for a cross-country haul. He has to move this mixed freight through bush, farmland and desert to Darwin.

programme.title: Trucking Hell
programme.desc: Super-sized transport jobs require even bigger trucks to perform them. Lifting the lid on the heavy recovery business, featuring industrial strength trucks capable of towing and lifting the biggest and heaviest machines on the nations roads.

programme.title: Trucking Hell
programme.desc: Recovery ops are dispatched to a toppled trailer and tractor unit on the busy A1. And in Walsall Snods deals with a car is on the brink of tumbling into a canal lock! (S9 Ep1)

programme.title: Casualty 24/7: Every Second Counts
programme.desc: The doors of Barnsley Accident and Emergency department are open every day. An exclusive look into what is really going on in the hearts and minds of the medical emergency teams and how they deal with the critically ill.

programme.title: Skin AandE
programme.desc: Both Dr Emma McMullen and Dr Adil Sheraz need a wipe down after dealing with cysts, Dr Toby Nelson has a lipoma named after him and Dr Natalia Spierings performs a mole removal.

programme.title: Eggheads
programme.desc: The Eggheads team are made up of some of Britains top quizzers. They face a team of challengers trying to beat the incumbents in order to win a cash prize. If the Eggheads beat the challengers, the cash prize is rolled over to subsequent episodes.

programme.title: Bargain-Loving Brits in the Sun
programme.desc: Caravan sales queen Bambi organises a beach-themed birthday for her friend Jojo on the Costa del Sol, while Diane and Jim host a British Legion fundraiser in Benidorm.

programme.title: Bargain-Loving Brits in the Sun
programme.desc: Former lorry driver Shane has plans for his Costa Del Sol bar with his ex Chrissy. Meanwhile, Paul takes on a catering job for a venues 20th anniversary in Benidorm.

programme.title: Dogs Behaving (Very) Badly
programme.desc: A wayward Jack Russell is ruining the relationship between a little girl and her grandad. And Graeme Hall revisits a couple whose pair of puppy sisters have returned to warring ways.

programme.title: Traffic Cops
programme.desc: In York, early-morning raids are carried out on two properties seeking suspects who are believed to be exploiting teenage girls. At Wetherby services on the A1M, seven stowaways are found in a lorry that has recently arrived from the continent.

programme.title: Traffic Cops
programme.desc: A tyre stinger is used to stop a vehicle suspected of carrying stolen mobile phones. Automatic Number Plate Recognition is used to track and intercept a vehicle suspected of being used by burglars targeting the homes of older folk.

programme.title: Traffic Cops
programme.desc: A van driver is twice the legal limit and has three bags of white powder in his pocket.

programme.title: Police Interceptors
programme.desc: The hunt for a suspected burglar takes an unexpected turn, a late-night stop reveals a bag of drugs and Jon has a tussle with a fallen tree. Barry deals with a case of some very hot dogs locked inside a car.

programme.title: Police Interceptors
programme.desc: Dan is in hot pursuit of a two-wheeled tearaway, a suspected drunk driver nearly causes a calamity and Mark has to turn Colombo at a three-car collision. Meanwhile, Sarah has a communication meltdown with a Mercedes driver.

programme.title: Police Interceptors
programme.desc: In this episode, dog handler Martyn finds himself in the firing line when he cops a tasty right hook, Dan is given the runaround by an uninsured driver and Jon deals with a shoplifter who has been caught by members of the public.

programme.title: Car Pound Cops: Give Me My Car Back!
programme.desc: Following the dramatic work of recovery crews as they trace, track and tow vehicles driven illegally on Britains roads. With access to two recovery companies, featuring the action from the moment they are called in, to the trucks arriving on scene.

programme.title: Home and Away
programme.desc: Sonnys changed his emergency contact to Remi after breaking up with Dana, but Remi had no idea.

programme.title: Home and Away
programme.desc: Eden is supportive of Remi as they prepare for his first radiation treatment and assures him shell be by his side through everything - including when hes throwing his guts up.

programme.title: GPs: Behind Closed Doors
programme.desc: Leslie has suffered tummy pains for years, and Dr Pearce suspects chicken nuggets are to blame. Lucy worries that her bloated stomach is ovarian cancer, but Dr Morris thinks the culprit is more likely to be broccoli.

programme.title: Call the Bailiffs: Time To Pay Up
programme.desc: Mark and Sam chase a £7,000 debt over a car sold with disputed mileage, leading to seized luxury cars, tense negotiations, and damage claims to an agents vehicle.

programme.title: About a Boy
programme.desc: Parallel coming-of-age stories of a layabout `serial nice guy living a posh, carefree lifestyle off his deceased fathers fortune, and a bright but awkward youth whos tired of his mothers depressed, boyfriend-less state. Their paths collide when the boy fabricates a two-year-old son and joins a group called S.P.A.T. (Single Parents Alone Together).

programme.desc: A man is brutally beaten to death in a Lancashire basement squat in the early hours. With the only suspect denying any involvement, can detectives separate the truth from the lies?

programme.desc: After murdering her parents, Virginia lived with their bodies almost five years. Features exclusive letters from Virginia as well as expert interviews and first-hand accounts from the local community.

programme.desc: Eilidh visits Angus and his four handsome and fun-loving cats. We find out how to look after cats and about all the lovely stuff Angus, Jasper, Parsley, Roxy and Dylan get up to together.

programme.title: Na Clangers
programme.desc: Tha sìol bho chraobh gu math sònraicht air tuiteam às na speuran. Ach de th ann? Seed from a very special tree has fallen from the sky. But what is it?

programme.title: Na Clangers
programme.desc: Tha Màidsear air a bheò ghlacadh leis an tunail annasach a tha d air lorg. Major is fascinated with the curious tunnel theyve discovered.

programme.title: S E Iasg a ThAnnam
programme.desc: A jellyfish explains how he moves around the sea without a skeleton, heart or brain. He also demonstrates how he uses his tentacles to catch his food and ward off predators.

programme.title: Stòiridh
programme.desc: Morag Ann MacNeil reads "Chan Fhaic Cearcan san Dorchadas".

programme.title: Clann Na Cruinne
programme.desc: Although Vito is nervous about his first fight, his coach and grandmother encourage him greatly. He wears a t-shirt that his mother owned and his grandmother is certain this will secure a win.

programme.title: Flix
programme.desc: Flix is a pug dog born to cat parents and living in Cat Town. Together with his best friends, Tabi and Munchkin, he climbs trees, speaks two languages, swims in the river, and explores all the marvels of Splitt Island.

programme.title: Seonaidh
programme.desc: Timmy decides to help the hedgehogs find a resting place for the night but ends up leading them into a series of scrapes.

programme.title: Bogaisean is Gumbalan
programme.desc: When the gumbles are attacked by one of Smiggless dream creatures, Merri and Jolli wake Smiggles up and get captured in the process. Tink decides to impersonate a Smiggles dream creature to save his friends.

programme.title: Slighe Chladach Fiobha
programme.desc: Iagan finds out why the area is synonymous with golf and sees the magnificent Rock and Spindle sea stack. He also finds out about the project to restore the sand dunes at the famous West Sands beach in St Andrews, plus looks at the Tay Bridges up close and visits Lindores Abbey and Distillery.

programme.title: SpeakGaelic
programme.desc: Joy Dunlop guides viewers through the first steps to inspire people to speak Gaelic. She introduces words and phrases on a variety of everyday topics. A Gaelic learner herself, Joy will support viewers through pronunciation and vocabulary.

programme.title: An La
programme.desc: Local, national and international news from BBC ALBA.

programme.title: Rùm Gu Rùm, Le Erica
programme.desc: Erica visits her home island of Skye for a barn renovation, and a stately home near Elgin once connected to cashmere wool.

programme.title: Miorbhail na Monach - Secrets in the Peat
programme.sub-title: Beatha air a Mhòintich - Life on the Peat
programme.desc: In Harris we go in search of the stags which roam the peaty uplands. And in Lewis, presenter Anne McAlpine joins Danny Mackay to put the finishing touches to a peat stack.

programme.desc: Tha Abigail a dèanamh Overnight Oats le Sùbhagan is Piostaisean. Abigail makes raspberry and pistachio overnight oats.

programme.title: Fred Dibnahs Magnificent Monuments
programme.desc: This film celebrates the grandeur of Hampton Court Palace and visits Cragside, one of the first homes to have electric lighting. Fred Dibnah also examines restoration work on a 15th century manor house.

programme.title: Wainwrights Walks: Coast to Coast
programme.desc: Richmond is the starting point for the longest section of Julias adventure, a town which Wainwright enjoyed. Miles of flat walking await as Julia sets off across the broad vale between the Dales and her last National Park. The reward is the rugged escarpment of the North York Moors, offering the broadest views and the finest weather of the journey.

programme.title: My Garden of a Thousand Bees
programme.desc: Wildlife film-maker Martin Dohrn is bee obsessed. He has found over 60 species in his Bristol garden, from giant bumblebees to scissor bees the size of a mosquito, which he films with mind-blowing results. Dohrn can identify individual insects and forms a remarkable relationship with one leaf-cutter bee he follows through its entire life.

programme.title: One Hundred Great Paintings
programme.desc: An introduction to John Stubbss 18th-century masterpiece The Grosvenor Hunt. Written and narrated by John Jacob.

programme.title: Secrets of Size: Atoms to Supergalaxies
programme.desc: Jim encounters ever larger cosmic structures to reveal the latest breakthroughs in understanding of the universe. For example, theres the heliosphere, a vast cloud of solar plasma that surrounds and protects the solar system. Its unique physics help people understand why life is possible. Larger still, Jim comes face to face with our galactic home the Milky Way, a monstrous structure sculpted by the gravitational forces of dark matter.

programme.title: This Life
programme.desc: Egg takes on a medical negligence case and becomes emotionally involved with a client who has cancer. Meanwhile, Warren discovers the truth about Delilah, and Miles chooses a bad person in whom to confide about his love life.

programme.title: This Life
programme.desc: Delilah outstays her welcome, Anna resorts to devious means to get her way, and Warren loses his cool over a muesli yoghurt.

programme.title: Vasa: The Ghost Ship
programme.desc: The Vasa, one of the most majestic galleons ever put to sea, sank 18 minutes after leaving Stockholm harbour on her maiden voyage in 1628. Following the work of archaeologists, divers and historians as they investigate the sinking.

programme.title: The Girl Who Talked to Dolphins
programme.desc: Documentary telling the story of the most extraordinary experiment in the history of animal science. In the 1960s a powerful and charismatic scientist flooded a house. He then invited a young woman to live there full-time with a dolphin.

programme.title: Just One Thing
programme.desc: In Bristol, distillery manager Luke is relying on caffeine to keep stress at bay - but Clive Myrie has a gentler, smarter swap in mind. If Luke traded his coffee and energy drinks for tea, the benefits could be huge. Studies show that drinking black tea regularly can reduce anxiety, calm the brain and even support heart health. A simple brew with surprisingly powerful perks.

programme.sub-title: Play-Off Semi-Final: Italy v Northern Ireland
programme.desc: Stephen Watson presents live coverage of the World Cup play-off semi-final between Italy and Northern Ireland from Bergamo. Former Northern Ireland internationals Stuart Dallas, Chris Brunt and Stephen Craigan are joined by Italian legend Gianfranco Zola as part of the BBC Sport NI team.

programme.title: Reporting Scotland
programme.desc: The latest news and weather from around Scotland.

programme.title: Roaming in the Wild
programme.desc: Mark and Andrew head to Assynt in the north of Scotland to seek out the Northern Lights. The weather has other plans, however, and their dreams look out of sight, so a plan B is considered instead.

programme.title: The Singer Story: Made in Clydebank
programme.desc: The Singer sewing machine, international icon and a symbol of the Industrial Revolution, helped to put the town of Clydebank on the map. Over the course of a century, it employed tens of thousands of `bankies before its demise in 1980.

programme.title: Debate Night
programme.desc: Topical debate on the big issues affecting Scotland and beyond, with members of the public putting their questions to a panel of politicians and other public figures about the biggest stories and the things they care about the most.

programme.title: River City
programme.desc: When Amber neglects The Oyster, a concerned Lydia takes matters into her own hands. Eddie discovers Francis nefarious intentions, Maggie is exasperated as she tries to rein in a jealous Caitlin, and Viv oversteps when she tries to uphold her end of the deal with Alex.

programme.title: Jules and Gregs Wild Swim
programme.desc: Heading to Scotlands southern edge, Jules and Greg brave the chilly waters of Garlieston Bay and Mossyard Beach, before finishing at the Mull of Galloway Lighthouse, Scotlands most southerly point.

programme.title: Scotcast
programme.desc: Conversation about Scotlands biggest news stories, hosted by Martin Geissler.

programme.title: Not Your Average Family
programme.desc: Charlie and Lauren deal with difficult fertility news, but with a business to open in a matter of months, there is no time to delay. Its all hands on deck to launch their safe space salon, amidst a backdrop of rising tension over trans rights in the UK, as Westminster blocks the Scottish Gender Recognition Reform Bill.

programme.title: Film Club
programme.desc: While Noa attends a taster day for his new job in Bristol, Evie pulls out all the stops for Film Club, theres a lot riding on tonight.

programme.title: Film Club
programme.desc: Evies still reeling from the message she saw on Noas phone. But when the two are left alone together, something extraordinary happens. Is it the start of a new journey for Evie?

programme.title: This Is BBC THREE

programme.title: This Is BBC THREE
programme.desc: BBC THREE: Refreshing TV for the digital age. Tune in daily from 7.00pm.

programme.title: The Catch-Up
programme.desc: A round-up of the days news.

programme.title: Deadly 60
programme.desc: Steve demonstrates how reading the tracks and signs left by an animal can lead to fantastic encounters. Lions, octopuses and badgers all feature in this special as Steve and his trusty crew travel the planet reading the signs they leave behind.

programme.title: Deadly 60
programme.desc: Go behind the scenes to see just how this series is filmed. From where the team eat and sleep, to the extreme measures they have to take as they travel the world filming some of its deadliest predators, this special tells all.

programme.title: Top Gear
programme.desc: The lads demonstrate that there most definitely is still such thing as a `bad car, and Jeremy makes the case for the Ford Mustang as the greatest car of all time. Also, TV cop icon and pop balladeer David Soul is the Star in a Reasonably Priced Car.

programme.title: The Apprentice
programme.desc: This week, the candidates are off to the Isle of Wight to find nine items associated with the area, and the team that spends the least money on the correct items wins. Following a dismal performance on the same task in Hong Kong, its a chance for the candidates to redeem themselves. A mad dash to the finish ensures a tight race, and Lord Sugar bids farewell to another candidate.

programme.title: Boarders
programme.desc: The boarders try to impress when they meet the parents - but will they succeed? Abby is confronted by Tobys mum about his future, and Cheddars dad gives Femi a daring challenge.

programme.title: Boarders
programme.desc: Femi is determined to take Cheddar down in the school elections, but at what cost? Leah is excited about Benjamin, but Felix gets in the way, and Toby has girlfriend troubles.

programme.title: The Trials of Pa Salieu
programme.desc: After prison, Coventry musician Pa Salieu returns with renewed purpose and takes the stage at Glastonbury. While he fights to rebuild his career, can music save him again?

programme.title: Top Gear
programme.desc: The lads demonstrate that there most definitely is still such thing as a `bad car, and Jeremy makes the case for the Ford Mustang as the greatest car of all time. Also, TV cop icon and pop balladeer David Soul is the Star in a Reasonably Priced Car.

programme.title: P Diddy: The Rise and Fall
programme.desc: Yinka Bokinni investigates how Sean Combs has gone from being the worlds biggest hip-hop mogul to haemorrhaging money, businesses, friends and fans in a matter of months. The worlds biggest hip-hop moguls legacy is now indelibly tarnished.

programme.title: Funboys
programme.desc: The three funboys, Callum Brown, Jordan McCafferty and Lorcan Boggin, may have taken the scenic route through emotional development but theyre trying to make the most of life in rural Ballymacnoose. While working at the local shop, Callum acquires his first-ever female admirer in the form of Gemma, who is new to town and seeking friends. Unfortunately, Callums burgeoning relationship means that he no longer wants to play video games all weekend with the fellas.

programme.title: Funboys
programme.sub-title: Once Upon a Time in Ballymacnoose
programme.desc: After a gas leak at his flat, Callum moves in with Lorcan and his dad, Sammy, aka the biggest git in Ballymacnoose. Driven by a desire to no longer be a pushover and in a desperate attempt to get over his break-up with Gemma, Callum goes under Sammys wing and unleashes his own inner git. Meanwhile, Jordans two dads are finally tying the knot and Jordan is caught up in all the excitement of the wedding.

programme.title: Funboys
programme.sub-title: Once Upon a Time in Ballymacnoose

programme.title: Inside the Rage Machine
programme.desc: The inside story of how social media algorithms have been helping to tear people apart. Insider testimony and documents expose a machine thriving on outrage and division.

programme.title: This Is BBC TWO
programme.desc: Preview of upcoming programmes from BBC Two.

programme.title: Just One Thing
programme.desc: In Sheffield, empty nesters Siva and Dhami are feeling stuck in a rut. But Roman Kemp is about to shake things up. He wants to show them how taking up dancing could do far more than just put a spring in their step. Research reveals that adding dance to your weekly routine can boost fitness, increase brain power and lift your mood. A little rhythm might just rewrite Siva and Dhamis whole routine.

programme.title: The Repair Shop: On the Road
programme.desc: Dom and Henry restore a century-old pump trolley, Julyan repairs a treasured guitar, and Sonnaz crafts a unique ring, using an intricate Japanese metalwork technique.

programme.title: Escape to the Country
programme.desc: Jules Hudson has a line-up of beautiful Cambridgeshire houses for a retired couple who want to relocate to be closer to family. Jules also visits the inspiring Great Fen Project.

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.sub-title: North East England and Yorkshire: Starter and Fish
programme.desc: Four chefs compete for north east England and Yorkshire with canapes, starters and fish dishes inspired by the British film industry. Who will be eliminated?

programme.title: BBC News
programme.desc: Twenty-four hours a day, the latest national and international stories as they break.

programme.title: Politics Live
programme.desc: Engaging conversation about the political issues that matter to you.

programme.title: Impossible
programme.desc: Thirty players compete across the series, scoring points by answering questions correctly. However, they must avoid the impossible answers, otherwise they will be eliminated from the show until the next day.

programme.title: The Tournament
programme.desc: Eight contestants compete in a series of fast-paced quiz battles in an attempt to eliminate their opponents from the days game and walk away with a cash prize.

programme.title: The Travelling Auctioneers
programme.desc: Izzie finds remarkable memorabilia and JJ restores a sideboard as they help a downsizing couple clear out 40 years of collectables following a life-altering diagnosis.

programme.title: Escape to the Country
programme.desc: Alistair Appleton heads to Devon, with a mother-and-daughter duo hopeful of finding a new countryside home for the whole family in or near Dartmoor National Park. Business potential is at the top of the wish list, as there are ambitions to create a retreat centring on their love of horses and yoga.

programme.title: The Repair Shop: On the Road
programme.desc: Dom and plaster expert Rich try to preserve some World War II Polish art before it is lost forever. Meanwhile, David Burville repairs a clockwork ship, and Will gets to grips with shinty.

programme.title: Antiques Roadshow
programme.desc: From the University of Birmingham, a Victorian painting damaged in the Blitz, a specially awarded telescope, an early 19th-century wooden ark filled with animals, and a small pottery Turks head - the most valuable piece of English pottery ever found at a Roadshow.

programme.title: Flog It!
programme.desc: Today Flog It! comes from Oxburgh Hall in Norfolk. Six antiques experts are on hand to find a selection of objects to take to auction. Anita Manning discovers a Victorian dynamometer used for measuring the pressure of steam.

programme.title: Richard Osmans House of Games
programme.desc: Former winners Amy Gledhill, Jacqui Joseph, Laurence Llewelyn-Bowen and Tim Vine test their general knowledge skills on this weeks Richard Osmans House of Games: Champions.

programme.title: Do You Know Your Place?
programme.desc: Vernon Kay teams up with unreliable tour guide Paul Gorton to see if Susan Calman, Max Fosh and Noreen Khan can separate the credible from the questionable in Leicester.

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.sub-title: North East England and Yorkshire: Main and Dessert
programme.desc: The remaining three chefs from north east England and Yorkshire compete over mains and desserts inspired by the British film industry. Who will win, and who must go home?

programme.title: Digging for Britain
programme.desc: The north of England is so rich in archaeological finds that Alice Roberts is travelling back there once again to reveal more of its fascinating history. She starts this journey at that most spectacular of Roman monuments, Hadrians Wall.

programme.title: QI XL
programme.desc: A quiz which compensates for the level of difficulty by awarding points, not just for correct answers but also for `interesting answers, regardless of whether or not they even have anything to do with the original question asked.

programme.title: Captive Audience: A Real American Horror Story
programme.desc: In 1972, seven-year-old Steven Stayner vanishes from Merced, California. His family struggles to keep his case in the public eye, until his heroic return seven years later sparks a media sensation.

programme.title: Newsnight
programme.desc: Featuring extensive coverage of the days top national and international news stories, along with explanations about the context and potential aftereffects.

programme.title: BBC Weather
programme.desc: The latest weather forecast.

programme.title: Unspun World with John Simpson
programme.desc: John Simpson brings his expertise to bear on the most important stories. In discussion with the BBCs range of experts across the world, he uses their unique local knowledge to go beyond the headlines to get to the unspun truth about the world.

programme.title: The Duchess
programme.desc: Following the tumultuous life of the 18th-century Duchess of Devonshire, a bright, popular aristocrat who is trapped in a failed marriage and has to face personal conflicts - including living with her husbands mistress and falling passionately in love with a young politician. Based on Amanda Foremans bestselling novel `Georgiana, Duchess of Devonshire.

programme.title: Europe on the Edge with Katya Adler
programme.desc: Katya continues her journey through Spain, training with a team of firefighters, and heads to France, witnessing a rocket launch in French Guiana and learning about Frances welfare state in Paris.

programme.title: The Walsh Sisters
programme.desc: Claire accidentally lets something slip. Rachel is forced to face some uncomfortable truths. Mammy spots Helen in town, and a fiery showdown erupts.

programme.desc: Karen gets a computer virus, and it is up to SpongeBob to try and get it out of her.

programme.title: Dennis and Gnasher Unleashed!
programme.desc: Tricked into a town-wide clean-up, Dennis and Walter are paired up against their will. However, they are forced to cooperate to survive in the wilderness of Beanotown Woods.

programme.title: Danny and Mick
programme.desc: Its Dannys turn to clean the gutters. Unfortunately, Danny is not too keen, and turns up in a full-body plaster cast in order to get out of the work.

programme.title: Bitesize: 7-9 Year Olds
programme.desc: Learn about magnetic force, push-and-pull forces, and how magnets are used.

programme.title: One Zoo Three
programme.desc: The brothers learn about animals that like to take things slowly. They discover the top speed of a giant African land snail and see just how slow a sleepy sloth can get.

programme.title: Horrible Histories
programme.desc: Terrible Tudors King Henry VIII and his daughter Queen Elizabeth I bicker about who was the best ruler. Henry had a mechanical dragon kite, but Liz had a flushing toilet.

programme.title: Shark Bites
programme.desc: Steve Backshall meets the hammerhead shark, a shark with some seriously super senses. He heads to his shark lab to reveal how their bizarrely shaped head allows them to detect electrical fields given off by their prey.

programme.title: Art Ninja
programme.desc: Rickys aunt Gloria has sent him a beautiful antique ornament, but its basically disgusting so he cant wait to get rid of it. It does inspire him to make some timeless art though, a gargoyle doorstop, an ancient Egyptian mask and a visit to Bristol Museum and Art Gallery leads to art thats classic jurassic.

programme.title: Horrible Science
programme.desc: Rapping science teacher Matt Green becomes the first human being ever to visit Quark as he joins Pipette on her home planet to help host this special episode about Earth and space.

programme.title: Deadly 60
programme.desc: Steve heads into the blistering sun of the outback to fulfil a lifelong dream of finding the most venomous snake in the world. Then the crew head to Tasmania where Steve finds a devilish mammal that will fly into a maniacal rage when competing for food and finally he goes in search of a toxic terror living right in the heart of Sydney, diving into the harbour to hunt for the Blue Ringed Octopus.

programme.title: Operation Ouch!
programme.desc: Things get explosive when Dr Chris and Dr Xand look at how spots are made, plus an incredible emergency service at work in Australia: the Flying Doctors.

programme.title: Sketchy Comedy
programme.desc: A pond, door, car and banana are among the items getting the Sketchy treatment!

programme.desc: Tee is excited about a day on the beach but soon finds out that the sea is a bit wet. Its now up to Mo to teach Tee that splashing in the water is fun.

programme.title: Supertato
programme.desc: Follow the adventures of Supertato, the worlds first potato superhero as he tackles the mischievous Evil Pea, in the supermarket aisles.

programme.title: Bing
programme.desc: Bing is teaching Charlie how to throw, but when he throws the ball in the lounge it knocks the fruit over! Bing starts to explain, but Charlie interrupts, leaving Flop to believe Charlie did it. Bing happily shows Charlie how to voosh Squeaky instead, but it flies out of his hands and hits Flops tray of snacks! After some encouragement, Bing tells Flop the truth and apologises.

programme.title: Mojo Swoptops
programme.desc: Mojo and Bo only wanted to help fix a satellite dish with Mojos Chopper Top. Now theyve been mistaken for a UFO, and the whole town is in a tizzy about invading aliens!

programme.title: Hey Duggees Squirrel Club
programme.desc: Today at Squirrel Club, Duggee forgets how to woof, Duggly comes to visit, Roly celebrates Potato Time, and Billy teaches everyone the Bugatron dance!

programme.title: Hey Duggee
programme.desc: Duggee is a big, friendly dog who runs The Squirrel Club, an after-school activity club where children can learn and take part in all kinds of activities, have fun adventures, and earn badges for their achievements.

programme.title: Bluey
programme.desc: Mackenzie, Jack and Rusty are playing as astronauts on a mission to Mars, but during their journey, Mackenzie keeps going missing and no one knows why, not even Mackenzie.

programme.title: Bluey
programme.desc: The Heelers go on a fun trip to Hammerbarn to get a new pizza oven like their neighbours. At Hammerbarn, Bluey and Bingo play in the gardening section and set up mini-gardens in the trolley.

programme.title: Go Jetters
programme.desc: Lars sees a mysterious creature near the Academy jungle and rushes to tell the others. Ubercorn explains that new species are discovered all the time, so this could be something that no one has seen before. He also tells them that new species are often named after the person who finds them. Excited by the prospect of lending their name to this mysterious creature, the Go Jetters, Grandmaster Glitch and Tala head out on a jungle expedition, but something seems to be watching them.

programme.title: Peter Rabbit
programme.sub-title: The Tale of the Locked Cage
programme.desc: When Mr McGregor locks him in a cage with Sammy Whiskers, Peter must join forces with the rat to escape.

programme.title: Octonauts: Above and Beyond
programme.desc: The Octonauts expand their exploration beyond the sea - and onto land. With new rides and new friends, they are prepared to protect any habitats and animals at risk on land or under the water.

programme.title: Numberblocks
programme.desc: Two finds a pair of magic dancing shoes and shows One that everything is better with two, singing and counting things that belong in pairs.

programme.title: Alphablocks
programme.desc: K can kick but C cant, so its word-magic-for-goalposts and a kid keeping score. Then they team up for CK vs The Sock, and they just need that magic touch.

programme.title: Love Monster
programme.desc: Love Monster cant find his favourite book that he borrowed from Book Cub, so he and Tiniest Fluffiest Bunny play detective and retrace his steps to try and find it.

programme.title: Playtime Towers
programme.desc: Bella and Lift enjoy making funny honk noises with tubes. They visit Tincy the Spider, who uses terrific tubes to make music with the Playtimers.

programme.title: Roots and Fruits
programme.desc: Live from the Roots and Fruits theatre, its love and peace symbol Lychee performing his song `All You Need Is Lychee.

programme.title: Bing
programme.desc: Bing and Sula are painting in the garden. When Bing cleans his brush and accidentally knocks the water over, he goes to fill it up from the garden tap. But he turns the water on too fast, making the hose voosh up into a big snake of water that covers Bing and Sula in wet paint! Flop shows Bing how to turn the tap on slowly.

programme.title: Yakka Dee
programme.desc: Dee encourages a friend to say `rain and goes on a whirlwind word adventure. Learn all about rain: drops of rain, trickly rain, pitter-patter rain, rainbow rain and really big splasher rain.

programme.title: Lu and the Bally Bunch
programme.desc: Lu and Barnaby have a disagreement during art time at Shell School.

programme.title: Piggy Builders
programme.desc: Three bunnies want to become builders, so the Piggies start teaching them. Though experts at building, the Piggies arent great teachers. The lessons quickly get messy and chaotic!

programme.title: Twirlywoos
programme.desc: The Twirlywoos are in a kitchen watching a man make a cup of tea. As he waits for the kettle to boil, he starts tapping out a rhythm with his spoon on the cup, gradually getting louder and louder. The Twirlywoos join in.

programme.title: Something Special
programme.desc: Meet some cute alpacas with Justin and his friend as they visit a farm, while Mr Tumble, Grandad Tumble and Aunt Polly sing Old MacDonald at the farmyard dance.

programme.title: Teletubbies
programme.desc: In Tummy Tales, a child makes party invitations, and in Teletubbyland a magical party invitation arrives. Its an invitation for the Teletubbies to attend the Tiddlytubbies party.

programme.title: The Baby Club
programme.desc: Giovanna and Baby Bear welcome babies Reau, Alfie, Charlie, Ollie, Otis, River and their grown-ups to The Baby Club. Inside Giovannas bag is a night light, and all the parents and babies have toys that could be used as part of a bedtime routine.

programme.title: I Spy, You Spy
programme.desc: Elsee plays I Spy on a day out at the beach with her friends Indie and Rosie.

programme.title: I Can Grow It
programme.desc: Adrian, Mia and Aaron go on a tree-mendous garden adventure to discover the magical powers of trees.

programme.title: Monster Makes
programme.desc: Rebecca is keen to go on a travelling adventure and invites Murphy to come. Murphys time machine takes them to the dinosaur age, but when it stops working, they need to get making. Firstly, they create a paper plate dinosaur, followed by some colourful water blob creatures made by the Mini Monsters. Then, Rebecca gets a call on the Magic Monster Mirror from her aunt Antarctic, who makes herself some snow-building buddies to help rescue her snowman.

programme.title: Vegesaurs
programme.desc: Ginger and the Peas help a lost Avocadodon find its perfect pair with a head-rubbing ritual.

programme.title: Bluey
programme.desc: A game in the park goes haywire when Bingo stops playing. Now Bluey must win her back.

programme.title: Hey Duggee
programme.desc: Duggee has a job to do outside of the Clubhouse, but who will look after the Squirrels? King Tiger of course, since he has his Babysitting Badge! But its actually the Squirrels who end up babysitting King Tiger.

programme.title: Hey Duggee
programme.sub-title: Top of the Pups: Here We Go Round the Mulberry Bush
programme.desc: Katerina Flamingo sings a song about routines in `Here We Go Round the Mulberry Bush.

programme.title: Lets Go for a Walk
programme.desc: Ranger Hamza and the Ramblers walk along a woodland trail to a place where they can play a game with sticks and stones. On the way, they find a tree monster tunnel, make wishes in a wishing well, count shapes in a little building and fly like owls!

programme.title: Our Family
programme.sub-title: Iona and Elbas Ice Cream Parlour
programme.desc: Iona and Elba create a pretend ice cream shop and make Mum a heart-shaped ice cream cake. Then its time for some real ice cream with sprinkles for decoration. On a family walk in the woods, Iona and Elba discover a trail of fairy clues to follow.

programme.title: My World Kitchen

programme.title: Teeny Tiny Creatures
programme.desc: Henry wants to know all about brilliant beetles. Chantelle is with farm vet Claire, looking for some teeny tiny creatures who love to recycle. Rory and his son Sandy are looking around the garden for little beetles and find a jumpy surprise.

programme.title: Dog Squad
programme.desc: Newfoundland Sylvie is a big therapy dog with big soft cuddles who loves to help people when theyre worried. She and her owner Katrina visit all sorts of people every day, young and old. Sylvie and Katrina are visiting some schoolchildren on their very first day at school. Sylvie knows the children will be feeling nervous and excited all at once.

programme.title: Go Wild
programme.sub-title: Aneeshwar and Lizzie: Coral and Flamboyant Flower Beetle
programme.desc: Aneeshwar and Lizzie see coral and meet flamboyant flower beetles.

programme.title: Waffle the Wonder Dog
programme.desc: Evie, Doug, Ruby and Ali rehearse for the school show. A big box arrives at Mrs Hobbs, and Waffle wants to know whats in it!

programme.title: Puffin Rock
programme.desc: Oona and Baba get lost in the fog. Mossy and the Shrew Crew help them to find their way home.

programme.title: Small Potatoes
programme.sub-title: You Can Be What You Want To Be
programme.desc: Animated series about a group of potatoes who sing songs in different genres. The Small Potatoes explore different jobs, from firemen to astronauts to movie stars, in this disco pageant.

programme.title: Yukee
programme.desc: Yukee enjoys the peace and quiet in the garden when she wakes up early. But she soon discovers that while it might be quiet, the garden is never silent.

programme.title: Hey Duggee
programme.desc: Duggee helps the Squirrels learn about all the amazing things trees do for the creatures who live amongst them, and earn their tree badges in the process.

programme.title: Yakka Dee
programme.desc: Dee encourages a friend to say `hair and we go on a whirlwind word adventure. We see various types of hair from curly to straight, brown to pink and even whizzy, whirly hair! We see cloudy hair and rainbow hair.

programme.title: Numberblocks
programme.desc: One to Fours pet bugs are racing on special tracks, but can they follow the perimeter?

programme.title: Alphablocks
programme.desc: When the Alphablocks make the alphabet, anything can happen - as PH, WH and a roller-skating elephant find out.

programme.title: Something Special

programme.desc: The Teletubbies become a pop group and perform a pop song. In Tummy Tales, children watch a one-man band playing music and accompany him by playing their own music.

programme.title: Patchwork Pals
programme.desc: Patchwork Chick has a problem: nobody wants to play with him because he is so small! But arent there even smaller Patchwork Pals around who also want to play?

programme.title: Raa Raa the Noisy Lion
programme.desc: Crocky has got a wobbly tooth. He really wants it to fall out. He and his friends try all sorts of different ways to try and help the tooth fall out.

programme.title: Friends
programme.desc: Feeling guilty for denying Chandler a bachelor party, Monica hires a stripper to perform for him, without learning that shes a prostitute. Elsewhere, Rachels father learns about her pregnancy and plans to give Ross a piece of his mind.

programme.title: Friends
programme.desc: An old high-school friend of Monicas is invited to the Thanksgiving dinner with the gang, but Monica is unaware that he isnt exactly fond of Rachel. Meanwhile, Chandler and Phoebe pretend to be mesmerised by American football in order to avoid helping Monica in the kitchen, and Joey vows to devour the entire turkey single-handedly.

programme.title: Friends
programme.desc: Chandler is aghast when Monica buys painfully expensive boots, and Phoebe tries to capitalise on her discovery that Ross son attends school with the son of rock star Sting.

programme.title: Friends
programme.sub-title: The One With Ross Step Forward
programme.desc: Ross new girlfriend wants to put their photograph on Christmas cards, Rachel eyes Joey as a possible romantic partner, and the newlyweds cope with Chandlers boss as he goes through a divorce.

programme.title: Friends
programme.sub-title: The One Where Joey Dates Rachel
programme.desc: To prepare Joey for his big date the following night, Rachel eagerly agrees to go out with him for a rare night on the town. However, when each is pleasantly surprised with their newfound compatibility, they begin to regard each other in a way that no one could have predicted.

programme.title: Friends
programme.sub-title: The One Where Chandler Takes a Bath
programme.desc: As Joey agonises over his secret affection for Rachel, Monica mistakenly thinks that he is infatuated with Phoebe. Meanwhile, Ross and Rachel promise not to try to find out the sex of their unborn child, but Rachel cant resist having a peek inside the doctors confidential folder. Also, Monica urges a reluctant Chandler to enjoy the soothing pleasures of a scented bath, but realises that shes created a tub monster.

programme.title: Friends
programme.sub-title: The One With the Secret Closet
programme.desc: Joey rushes pregnant Rachel to hospital when she experiences pains. When Ross feels left out, Joey makes a gallant suggestion. Meanwhile, Chandler is surprised to discover what is behind the locked door of Monicas secret cupboard, and Phoebe gets the impression that Monica is enjoying one of her massages a little too much.

programme.title: Friends
programme.sub-title: The One With the Birthing Video
programme.desc: Monica prepares for a sexy Valentines Day, but Chandler is too shell-shocked after accidentally viewing Rachels birthing video. Meanwhile, Phoebe gives Joey the worlds most cheerful dog.

programme.title: Friends
programme.sub-title: The One Where Joey Tells Rachel
programme.desc: A guilt-ridden Joey finally confesses his love for Rachel to the flabbergasted Ross, while Chandler becomes nervous when Phoebes handsome new boyfriend Don shares numerous personality traits with Monica.

programme.title: Friends
programme.sub-title: The One With the Tea Leaves
programme.desc: In an effort to restore normality to their friendship, Rachel tells Joey a big lie about her unborn child. Meanwhile, Phoebes tea leaves reveal that she will soon encounter the man of her dreams.

programme.title: Friends
programme.desc: Phoebe brings her annoyingly upbeat boyfriend to Mr and Mrs Gellers 35th wedding anniversary party. Meanwhile, Ross is astounded when his parents ask him and the pregnant Rachel to pretend to be married, in order to avoid a potential scandal.

programme.title: Friends
programme.desc: Joey is thrilled when his name is used in a `Soap Opera Digest crossword puzzle. He is reluctant to let the magazine do a profile of him, but Rachel tries to persuade him to reconsider so that he can mention her.

programme.title: Friends
programme.sub-title: The One With the Baby Shower
programme.desc: Rachel panics when she realises how unprepared she is to care for an infant. She reluctantly agrees to allow her frustrating mother Sandra to move in to help her, upsetting Ross. Meanwhile, Monica tries to atone for forgetting to invite Sandra to the baby shower, and Chandler and Ross exhaust themselves by repeatedly playing a confusing game called Bamboozle to help Joey prepare to audition as a game show host.

programme.title: Friends
programme.sub-title: The One With the Cooking Class
programme.desc: When Monicas cooking at the restaurant receives a harsh write-up in a newspaper, she goes to a criticism class taught by the reviewer, hoping to convince him to reconsider his opinion. Meanwhile, Rachel gets upset when a cute salesgirl flirts with Ross while he and Rachel are shopping for baby supplies, and nervous about a major job interview, Chandler receives some questionable coaching from Phoebe.

programme.title: Friends
programme.sub-title: The One Where Rachel Is Late
programme.desc: When Rachels pregnancy goes beyond the expected delivery date, the pain makes her increasingly irritable and willing to try anything that might speed up the babys arrival. Meanwhile, Monica and Phoebe bet on the date of the birth, while Joey can only take one guest to the premiere of his film, and chooses Chandler. Unfortunately, Chandler falls asleep during the screening.

programme.title: Deal or No Deal
programme.desc: Airing from 2005 to 2009 on NBC, the game show series returns to primetime TV with original host Howie Mandel. A contestant must choose from 26 sealed briefcases containing a marker for various amounts of cash -- from one penny to $1 million. The player then eliminates the remaining 25 cases one by one. The chosen ones are opened and the amount of money inside revealed. After several cases are opened, the player is tempted by the Banker to accept an offer of cash in exchange for not continuing the game and possibly winning a larger sum of money.

programme.desc: As Thanksgiving arrives at the Goldberg house, Pop-Pop and Uncle Marvin each find a way to disrupt Beverlys best-laid plans.

programme.title: Modern Family
programme.desc: Calamity and comedy go hand-in-hand when the Dunphys throw Luke a birthday bash. The party is certainly memorable, especially when Cam shows up as Fizbo the clown.

programme.title: Modern Family
programme.desc: This seasonal special reminds us that Christmas is about giggling as well as giving. Claire and Phil go all Grinch-y, and Lily is taken to see Santa.

programme.title: Married at First Sight Australia
programme.desc: The brides are torn over Stella and Brooks fallout as Intimacy Week kicks off, spelling disaster for one bride whose groom refuses to kiss her.

programme.title: First Dates
programme.desc: A new series begins with comedy queen Carol, 26-year-old Charles, whos never been kissed, and two south London sisters, both single, who are keen to do something about it.

programme.title: Ramsays Kitchen Nightmares USA
programme.desc: Gordon travels to Ventura Harbor in Southern California to help a family-run Greek restaurant facing big problems that hasnt taken a profit for two years. Makis Mikelatos and his wife Lynn have run the restaurant, the Greek at the Harbor, for 17 years, but Makis - who works 17 hours a day, seven days a week, and rarely takes a holiday - is exhausted, and if he doesnt slow down hes in danger of collapse.

programme.title: The Simpsons
programme.desc: Grandpa Simpson is moved out of the Springfield Retirement Castle and into the Simpson family home in a bid to save Homer cash. The move brings Grandpa closer to Selma and love blossoms between the unlikely pair. Meanwhile, Lisa and Bart cause mischief with a bunch of complimentary shipping boxes.

programme.title: The Simpsons
programme.desc: After experiencing the rush of selling a scandalous picture of Duffman cheating on his fiancee with Booberella to a sleazy tabloid, Homer decides to become a paparazzo.

programme.title: Modern Family
programme.desc: Phil and Jay take the reigns when Luke and Mannys hot-tempered basketball coach quits during a game. Meanwhile, Mitchell invites Cameron to meet a prospective employer.

programme.title: Modern Family
programme.desc: Claire is dubious of Phils father when he pays a visit with a dog and RV in tow. Meanwhile, Jay and Gloria take Manny to see a horror movie and Cameron fills a temporary void in Dylans band.

programme.title: Married at First Sight Australia
programme.desc: The brides are torn over Stella and Brooks fallout as Intimacy Week kicks off, spelling disaster for one bride whose groom refuses to kiss her.

programme.title: Celebrity Coach Trip
programme.desc: As the coach pulls into Portimao, two legendary famous faces hop aboard to join the fun. The celebs hang out with some furry friends as they try to master the art of dog training, before hitting the track for an afternoon of high-octane go-karting.

programme.title: The Simpsons
programme.desc: When Marge gets a new car that only seats five, she realises she might want to have a another baby. Meanwhile, Bart and his friends are determined to find out what secret Lisa is keeping.

programme.title: The Simpsons
programme.desc: Homer and Marge uncover unexpected details about Grampas past.

programme.title: Hollyoaks
programme.desc: Rex braves his first public outing while experimenting with his image at an LGBTQ+ club night. Meanwhile, a shocking turn of events at The Loft relaunch party puts all eyes on Mercedes.

programme.title: Married at First Sight Australia
programme.desc: Intimacy Week reaches boiling point as one relationship threatens to implode, while a bride makes a decision that leaves her groom heartbroken.

programme.title: Beyond Chelsea
programme.desc: Tensions rise between Lucy and Rosie at Lucys vegan cookbook launch. Binkys children star in the launch of her kids clothing line. And Ollie Locke dabbles in a bit of matchmaking.

programme.title: Gogglebox
programme.desc: Bob Mortimer, Richard Ayoade, Jennifer Saunders, Stephen Graham and Hannah Walters join the Goggleboxers for Survivor, Planet Earth III, Teen First Dates and loads more top TV.

programme.title: First Dates
programme.desc: The First Dates restaurant opens its doors to more hopeful singletons from across the country. Hugo returns for another shot at love. Trainee opera singer Olympia takes centre stage for Hugos encore performance.

programme.title: Naked Attraction
programme.desc: Gym addict Sophie from Surrey is looking for a change from the usual body-builder types who lack sparkle, while Dom seeks his perfect man to waltz him down the aisle.

programme.title: Beautiful Beings
programme.desc: Addi, a gifted boy raised by a clairvoyant mother, decides to adopt a bullied misfit into his gang of outsiders. Left to their own devices, the boys discover aggression, violence, loyalty, and love.

programme.title: Teleshopping
programme.desc: Shopping from home.

programme.title: Teleshopping
programme.desc: Shopping from home.

programme.title: River of No Return
programme.desc: Thriller set against the background of the gold rush in the Canadian Rockies. A man jailed for murder falls for the beautiful saloon singer who looked after his son while he was inside. He tries to conceal his criminal past from his son, but that is the least of their problems when the trio are left defenceless against rampaging Indians.

programme.title: The Enemy Below
programme.desc: During World War II, the commander of an American destroyer matches wits with a German U-boat captain in the Atlantic.

programme.title: The Sheriff of Fractured Jaw
programme.desc: Having inherited a failing gunsmith business, quintessential Englishman Jonathan Tibbs decides that the real market for his wares is in the Wild West. After falling foul of both cowboys and Indians, he finds himself conned into becoming the law-man in the lawless town of Fractured Jaw.

programme.title: The Northman Interview Special
programme.desc: `The Northman interview special.

programme.title: Harvey
programme.desc: Gentle family film about an eccentric drunk who befriends a six-foot white rabbit that only he can see, much to the embarrassment of his social-climbing sister who tries to have her brother committed.

programme.title: Made in Italy
programme.desc: A bohemian artist travels from London to Italy with his estranged son to sell the house they inherited from his late wife.

programme.title: Carlitos Way
programme.desc: A New York criminal is released after five years in prison when his corrupt lawyer exploits a legal technicality. Determined to go straight, he only agrees to take part in a shady business deal at his attorneys instigation in order to discharge the debt of gratitude he owes. Despite his intentions and love for his girlfriend, Carlito finds himself relentlessly drawn back into the world he sought to escape.

programme.title: Ready or Not
programme.desc: Grace couldnt be happier after she marries the man of her dreams at his familys luxurious estate. Theres just one catch - she must now hide from midnight until dawn while her new in-laws hunt her down with guns, crossbows and other weapons. As Grace desperately tries to survive the night, she soon finds a way to turn the tables on her not-so-lovable relatives.

programme.title: The Last Tree
programme.desc: After growing up in quiet, rural Lincolnshire, teenager Femi struggles with unfamiliar surroundings and cultural differences when he moves in with his mother in London.

programme.desc: Ree Drummond recreates a Tex-Mex dish from a friends engagement party. She makes crispy cerveza battered fish, with a fiery salsa roja and cooling charred lime crema, and prickly pear margaritas.

programme.desc: The bakers prepare yeast-leavened cakes, a Swedish princess torte and a contemporary version of the Dobos Torte. Whose `Bake Off will come to a sweet but sticky end?

programme.title: James Martins French Adventure
programme.desc: James arrives in the misty gardens of Claude Monets house in Giverny and walks across the iconic Japanese bridge to gaze at the water lilies made famous by the artist.

programme.title: James Martins French Adventure
programme.desc: Jamess adventure brings him to one of his favourite cities in the world. He has an early start, off to one of the largest wholesale food markets in Europe, Rungis.

programme.title: The Hairy Bikers Go North
programme.desc: This week, the Hairy Bikers are in Yorkshire. The plan is simple - riding through the county to visit top food producers, who not only have amazing products but equally amazing stories to tell.

programme.title: The Pioneer Woman
programme.desc: Ree Drummond is in the mood to create show-stopping pies. She shares a fantastical waffle cone ice cream pie and a decadent double chocolate pie.

programme.title: The Pioneer Woman
programme.desc: Ree makes care packages for her two daughters in college; she sends them homemade breakfast tarts, nut and seed brittle, salt and pepper crackers and cherry gummies; Rees sons get involved, too, shopping for fun treats to send to their sisters.

programme.title: Jamies Quick and Easy Food
programme.desc: Jamie Oliver makes high-speed ginger shakin beef, crispy squid and smashed avocado, pork and mash gratin, and almond, orange and polenta cake.

programme.title: Jamies Quick and Easy Food
programme.desc: Jamie Oliver makes a crazy good pork burger, sticky mango prawns, rustic gnocchi and frozen banoffee cheesecake.

programme.title: Barefoot Contessa
programme.desc: Ina is writing an article for Food Network magazine, and its all about cheese. Whether its cooked, raw, soft, hard or blue, Inas got cheese covered.

programme.title: Barefoot Contessa
programme.desc: Ina makes dinner to celebrate the arrival of her friends newborn son. Theres a delicious nursery food menu and an incredible setting to match. Theres Truffle Popcorn with drinks followed by Spicy Turkey Meatballs, Strawberry Rhubarb Crisp and Smores around the fire pit. The whole meal is served outside under incredible garden lights designed by Inas friend Greg Yale.

programme.title: The Great British Bake Off
programme.desc: The contestants bake sweet custard tarts, mini pear pies and finally a towering collection of pies from any pastry or pastries they like.

programme.title: The Great British Bake Off
programme.desc: The bakers prepare yeast-leavened cakes, a Swedish princess torte and a contemporary version of the Dobos Torte. Whose `Bake Off will come to a sweet but sticky end?

programme.title: James Martins Islands to Highlands
programme.desc: James is joined by Michelin-starred chef Kenny Atkinson, and they set out to sample the delights of Northumberland. They learn how mead is made on Holy Island, before James cooks up a Singapore-inspired crab and lobster dish on a stunning beach, and they both enjoy a Puffin cruise at Cockett Island. Theres a trip to a lobster hatchery too, before James cooks local speciality Singing Hinnies on another breathtaking beach.

programme.title: James Martins Islands to Highlands
programme.desc: James is joined by Michelin-starred chef Lisa Goodwin-Allen for a visit to the Isle of Man during the famous TT race warm-up week. James serves up proper biker food, pork burgers with a spicy apple sauce, while Lisa visits local organic juice producers. James picks up some local Manx kippers and makes a delicious soup, before meeting TT legend John McGuinness. Finally, he treats John and his team to some tasty lamb chops with hispi cabbage and an olive glaze.

programme.title: Diners, Drive-Ins and Dives
programme.desc: In San Diego, an all-day breakfast spot serving creme brulee French toast, in Portland, corned beef hash and chipped beef, in San Francisco, lamb merquez dog.

programme.title: Diners, Drive-Ins and Dives
programme.desc: In Portland, Ore., a chef who butchers, cures, preserves and smokes everything in-house, in San Carlos, Calif., a neighborhood spot featuring burgers, cheese steaks and signature sandwiches.

programme.title: Diners, Drive-Ins and Dives
programme.desc: Braised beef short ribs and spicy shrimp and gravy, chicken noodle with matzo ball soup and potato latkes, pork belly and short rib kojas.

programme.title: Diners, Drive-Ins and Dives
programme.desc: In Portland, Ore., shrimp fritters and a unique chicken salad, in Wheat Ridge, Colo., cannoli and kraut burgers, in San Francisco, adobo buffalo wings and pork tacos.

programme.title: Man v Food
programme.desc: A pitcher-sized Bloody Mary garnished with a whole chicken. A four-and-a-half-pound Reuben sandwich.

programme.title: Man v Food
programme.desc: A 151-ounce milkshake, an eight-pound sloppy joe or six habanero ghost chili wings.

programme.title: Cake Boss
programme.desc: Buddy creates a robot cake for a gala honouring an advanced robot. Plus, Mary helps a five-year old girls dream for a unicorn birthday cake come true.

programme.title: Cake Boss
programme.sub-title: Steampunks, Kiwi Cake and Babysitting Jitters
programme.desc: The team creates an amazing three-piece steampunk-inspired cake. Buddy makes a special cake for New Zealanders living in New York.

programme.title: Teleshopping
programme.desc: Shopping from home.
