programme.title: Clifford
programme.desc: Mr Howard is getting rid of something big and red. Could it be Clifford? Emily Elizabeth and Clifford use their sleuthing skills to solve the mystery.

programme.title: Clifford
programme.desc: Fire Chief Franklin rescues Fisherman Charlie and the kids honour him with a medal of bravery. But who will rescue the medal when it disappears?

programme.title: Clifford
programme.desc: Fisherman Charlies new puppy Hudson keeps saying mine!, so Clifford Bailey and Tucker start puppy school to teach him to be mindful of others.

programme.title: Tickety Toc
programme.desc: Tommy and Tallulah are trying to make everyone laugh, but McCoggins is not responding. The twins do everything they know to make him crack and eventually he laughs so loud that he gets a case of gigglitis, but McCoggins was working on a very delicate job at the clock and now they need to cure him in time for Chime Time.

programme.title: Tickety Toc
programme.desc: Tommy is desperate to get the hang of the game of Whackerty so that he can compete in the town tournament. Everyone has different and unusual playing styles, but when he tries to copy them the results are disastrous. Somehow he must develop his own style to win.

programme.title: Tickety Toc
programme.desc: Tickety legend has it that McCoggins is building an amazing model of the clockhouse out of lollipop sticks. No one has seen it yet and the whole town is waiting for the grand unveiling, but when McCoggins asks Tommy and Tallulah to help him finish his model, the twins accidentally smash it.

programme.title: Tickety Toc
programme.desc: Tommy and Tallulah have such fun with their new friend Larry the Bug that they decide to adopt him as a pet, but just as they finish making him a home, Larry flies away! After spending the day chasing him all over town, they are surprised to discover that Larry has a family of cute little baby bugs.

programme.title: Tickety Toc
programme.desc: Tommy and Tallulah enlist Battersby as their star performer for Show Time. Unfortunately, the magic trick they devise for him doesnt go to plan and soon Madame Au Lait is stuck down Hopparoos portable hole. Tallulahs trumpet is also trapped and she needs it for Chime Time.

programme.title: Tickety Toc
programme.desc: When food goes missing from all over Tickety Town, Tommy and Tallulah are determined to find the culprit, but as they follow the trail of thefts, their prime suspect keeps changing as each of them has also been burgled. Meanwhile, the clockhouse has been overrun by the Spring Chicks cousins who have come to stay.

programme.title: Tickety Toc
programme.desc: Tommy and Tallulah declare its `Camping Time and set off for the Gnarly Tree, but when the Spring Chicks tag along and pester them for their banana muffins, they move camp and forget to tell anyone where they have gone - causing a dreadful panic in Tickety town as Chime Time approaches.

programme.title: Tickety Toc
programme.desc: Everyone is in the town square swapping something they dont want for something they do. Hopparoo ends up with Tommys drum and loves it so much that he refuses to give it back, even with Chime Time looming! The only way he will release it is if the twins can find him a bigger, louder drum to play.

programme.title: 64 Zoo Lane
programme.sub-title: The Story of the House of Leaves
programme.desc: Nelson the elephant wants to build the biggest and best house of leaves ever. His nephew Nigel is eager to help out, but Nelson is worried that the leaf house might be too fragile for his exuberant young nephew. When the house of leaves comes crashing down, Nelson wastes no time in telling Nigel off.

programme.title: 64 Zoo Lane
programme.sub-title: The Story of Gertie the Goat
programme.desc: Gertie the goat has agreed to take Bao Bao the panda on a climbing expedition up the So Hi Mountains. The other animals advise Gertie against the trip, since pandas are not renowned for their climbing skills. Will Gertie and Bao Bao prove the others wrong, or will they need some help from their friends?

programme.title: 64 Zoo Lane
programme.sub-title: The Story of the Jungle Ball
programme.desc: The Jungle Ball is imminent and all the animals are busy finding their dance partners for the night. All except Reginald - nobody has ever asked him to dance with them at the Jungle Ball. He sets off in search of a dance partner, but has he left it too late?

programme.title: 64 Zoo Lane
programme.sub-title: The Story of the Great Rains
programme.desc: The savannah animals are busy preparing for the great rains, which are due any day. The rains flood the plains every year and they will have to take shelter on Blue Mountain. This year it is Giggles and Tickles turn to keep watch for the start of the rain.

programme.title: 64 Zoo Lane
programme.desc: Nelson is in an inventive mood, and agrees to make various objects for his friends. Alan the aardvark is excited about the scooter that Nelson is making him - even though he doesnt really know what a scooter is. The other animals all take it in turns to help deliver the scooter to Alan, each making their own modifications to the vehicle on the way. Alan is delighted when his very own scooter eventually arrives.

programme.title: 64 Zoo Lane
programme.sub-title: The Story of Reginalds New Look
programme.desc: Nathalie the antelope decides to give Reginald the lion a makeover to refresh his look. She restyles him to such an extent that none of the other animals recognise him when he tries to alert them of an imminent danger. Will Reginald be able to lead his friends to safety in time?

programme.title: 64 Zoo Lane
programme.desc: It is Washi-Washi day in the So Hi Mountains, when all the animals wash and groom themselves until they are squeaky and clean. While all the other animals are having fun getting clean, Horace the hare keeps making excuses not to join in. Can Horaces friends persuade him that bathing can be great fun?

programme.title: 64 Zoo Lane
programme.sub-title: The Story of the Last Buluru Berry
programme.desc: Joey and his friends are excited when they come across a bush full of delicious smelling buluru berries. Wally Two Trees warns them that they must leave the berries on the bush until they are all ripe, when there will be a grand tasting, but berries keep disappearing off the bush.

programme.title: 64 Zoo Lane
programme.desc: Doris the duck finds a plant promising beautiful flowers. She enlists Toby and Kevins help to protect it at all costs. Eventually Doris learns that flowers should be left for everybody to enjoy.

programme.title: Nothing to Declare

programme.title: Nothing to Declare
programme.desc: This episode, an unruly passenger has Customs concerned that they may be carrying something illegal.

programme.title: Nothing to Declare
programme.desc: This episode, a baby-faced bandit is caught by Immigration acting suspicious.

programme.title: Highway Patrol
programme.desc: More footage from Australia as we follow the men and women of the highways agencies as they patrol the countrys roads and freeways.

programme.title: Highway Patrol
programme.desc: A young motorcyclist executes a reckless one-wheel manoeuvre in front of Ash Bowdens unmarked police car, and finds himself in deeper trouble when he makes excuses and denies responsibility.

programme.desc: Follow US border protection agencies as they man the front lines of national security.

programme.desc: Follow Australian Customs and Border Protection officers as they attempt to enforce the countrys various quarantine, customs, immigration and finance laws.

programme.title: Star Trek: Deep Space Nine
programme.desc: The wife of a prominent expert on dead planet regeneration provides bittersweet mystery and heartache for Sisko.

programme.title: Stargate SG-1
programme.desc: Exposure to an alien device causes Col. ONeill and TealC to relive the same day over and over again.

programme.title: Star Trek: Voyager
programme.desc: Seven of Nine creates a holodeck fantasy where she can mingle with her colleagues, but she ends up falling in love with a senior crew member.

programme.title: Star Trek: The Next Generation
programme.desc: Riker is offered god-like powers in exchange for joining the Q continuum.

programme.title: Nothing to Declare
programme.desc: A nervous man claims he has arrived to visit his wife for their wedding anniversary but whats in his luggage may spell the end of his trip. A drug delivery is intercepted.

programme.title: Nothing to Declare
programme.desc: A man from Amsterdam says he is visiting for a holiday, but his suspicious travel itinerary through China and an empty suitcase seem to tell a very different story.

programme.title: Worlds Most Evil Killers
programme.desc: After a worker found the body of Jarrae Estepp at a California recycling centre, detectives closed in on her murderer, Steven Dean Gordon, and discovered that he had killed before.

programme.title: Killer In My Village
programme.desc: Peter Wrighton: The story of the senseless killing of 83-year-old Peter Wrighton, who was targeted by Alexander Palmer while out walking his dog in 2017.

programme.title: Star Trek: Enterprise
programme.desc: While stranded on an uninhabited planet, Trip, Mayweather and TPol are overcome by paranoid fantasies caused by a hallucinogenic released by flowers, and Archer must save them before they turn on each other.

programme.title: Warrior
programme.desc: Strickland instils confidence in Learys political abilities; tensions rise between Young Jun and Ah Sahm.

programme.title: The Force: Manchester
programme.desc: Fast-paced documentary series following the 999 work of Greater Manchester Police. Englands second largest police force, its responsible for protecting 2.7 million people.

programme.title: Brit Cops: Rapid Response
programme.desc: Following rapid response officers, the fastest cops in Britain, as they crack down on crime.

programme.title: Highway Patrol
programme.desc: More footage from Australia as we follow the men and women of the highways agencies as they patrol the countrys roads and freeways.

programme.title: Humanlike: The Chimps of Fongoli
programme.desc: Join anthropologist Jill Pruetz and guide Dondo (Johnny) Kante and watch evolution happen in real time. Meet the elusive chimps of Fongoli, Senegal, who use tools, interact with fire, and adapt to the human world as it collides with their own.

programme.title: Guardians of the Wild
programme.desc: When a trio of Elephants are orphaned in Zambia, they are rescued and transported to a purpose-build nursery. But it wont be smooth-sailing for the young Pachyderms; life lessons will be tough to imprint before they are returned to the wild.

programme.title: Orangutan Jungle School
programme.desc: A two-year-old orangutan who was drugged and kept as an illegal pet by an oil sheik arrives at jungle school. Plus, angry male orangutan Jerry has gone missing and has not been seen for 18 days.

programme.title: New Kids in the Wild

programme.desc: Taut Second World War film reflecting the experience of the generation of Australians that fought the war against the Japanese. Jean Paget is among a group of women trapped in Malaya who are forced to walk hundreds of miles in search of food and shelter by the Japanese army, but Australian soldier Joe Harman sneaks food to them from his labour camp. After the war, Jean visits Joe in his home town of Alice Springs.

He thinks hes found the perfect spot to pop the question - the bridge over the duck pond.

programme.title: WW1 Battles

programme.desc: Calum welcomes the new arrivals - one that bites and one that fits Siegfrieds domestic requirement. James, meanwhile, receives an invitation to examine Lord Buttermeres new stallion.

programme.desc: Tom is attacked by an intruder at the boatyard, while Gerald and Polly have a furious row and end their marriage.

programme.title: Shakespeare and Hathaway: Private Investigators
programme.sub-title: If Music Be the Food of Love
programme.desc: Frank and Lu enter the competitive world of ballroom dancing, brimming with passion and jealousy, and learn the dangers of falling in love with your dance partner.

programme.title: Birds of a Feather
programme.desc: A beautiful friendship begins for Sharon when she sees off a mugger who is attacking a businessman in Chigwell railway station. A chance encounter later in the evening reveals he is, in fact, Doriens husbands financial adviser.

programme.title: Last of the Summer Wine
programme.sub-title: Will Randolph Make a Good Impression?
programme.desc: Hobbo turns up at the river wall with Randolph, who he hopes will bring romance into Stellas life. Randolph seems to lack any of the requirements of a potential suitor, and is limp and unattractive, especially after Hobbo drops him in the river. After a little rehearsal and some invaluable tips on how to give Stella `the look, Randolph demonstrates that he has no chance whatsoever of impressing her.

programme.title: Last of the Summer Wine
programme.sub-title: In Which Romance Springs a Leak
programme.desc: Toby is beginning to realise that the benefits of going back to his ex-wife far outweigh the advantages of living alone in a sparsely furnished house with no-one to cook or clean for him. Fortunately, Hobbo is on hand to skilfully guide him on the route back to marital bliss. However, for some reason, his wife doesnt want him back, and flowers dont work.

programme.title: Last of the Summer Wine
programme.sub-title: Variations on a Theme of Fathers Day
programme.desc: In order that Hobbo can find out more about Nelly, his supposed mother, he has Howard eavesdrop on her when she is visiting Pearl. Soon realising what Howard is up to, the women plant a completely false story about Hobbos father being a hippy. Hobbo loses no time in making himself look exactly like the description of his father, complete with earrings and beads.

programme.title: The Marlow Murder Club
programme.sub-title: Murder in the Dark: Part One
programme.desc: When a man is killed at her university reunion, Becks finds herself on the wrong side of an investigation. Was a friend responsible or did someone break in?

programme.title: The Marlow Murder Club
programme.sub-title: Murder in the Dark: Part Two
programme.desc: As Judith and Suzie work with the police to find the killer, tensions rise among Becks friends in the house. Guest starring Harry Enfield.

programme.title: New Tricks
programme.desc: An eccentric group of ex-policemen are brought out of retirement to investigate unsolved crimes. Jack Halford decides to take revenge against his wifes killer by running him down, but luckily Pullman and the team crash into him before he succeeds. With all three men in hospital and awkward questions being asked, the boys decide to prove their worth by investigating the case of a patient who was murdered ten years previously.

programme.desc: Stacey spends the weekend with former glamour model Carla Bellucci, who built her career around creating controversial headlines about herself and her family.

programme.desc: Heroin addict Missy is visited by her sugar daddy, who gives her the cash she needs to fund her habit. The secret romance between inmates Franklin and Leslie continues to blossom.

programme.title: Prison Girls

programme.title: Louis Theroux Under the Knife
programme.desc: Louis Theroux pulls back the folds of plastic America and gets under the skin of the plastic surgery patients that crowd the clinics of Beverly Hills. Louis becomes a part of the plastic surgeons daily lives, joining them for their consultations and operations in the search for everlasting youth. As Louis tries to understand these motivations, he wonders if he too might be a candidate for a nip tuck.

programme.title: Louis Theroux: The Most Hated Family in America
programme.desc: Louis struggles to come to terms with the infamous Phelps family from Topeka, Kansas who picket the funerals of dead soldiers killed in Iraq as a protest against an America that tolerates homosexuality. The family members, some as young as six years old, wave slogans with messages such as `God Hates Fags, Thank God for 9/11, and God Hates You at pickets all over the States.

programme.title: Louis Theroux: Americas Most Hated Family in Crisis
programme.desc: Following up on his 2007 documentary, `The Most Hated Family in America, Louis Theroux returns to Topeka, Kansas, for a week-long visit with the Westboro Baptist Church. He again joins the Phelps family on their controversial pickets where they antagonise communities with offensive slogans. However, four years on from Louiss last visit, there are signs of disarray in the Phelps clan.

programme.title: Stacey Dooley
programme.desc: Stacey travels to America to meet people prepping for an uncertain future, all with different concerns - from climate change and natural disasters to civil unrest.

programme.title: DIY SOS: The Big Build
programme.desc: Nick Knowles and the team head to Bury to rebuild the home of the Malone family, whose daughter Maddie struggles to walk and talk.

programme.title: Tipping Point
programme.desc: Contestants play a large coin-drop machine, provided they answer questions correctly. Answer incorrectly and the players could be eliminated. The last contestant standing has an opportunity to win the machines £10,000 jackpot.

programme.title: My Dream Home
programme.desc: Brothers Drew and Jonathan assist couple Sandy and Pat, who want a contemporary home of their own. They are on the search for a home in Nashville, Tennessee, where they hope to put down roots.

programme.title: My Dream Home
programme.desc: After supporting their grandmother through a tough time, Carolyn, Brad and their family are now searching for a home that they can call their own and seek the help of the Drew and Jonathan.

programme.title: DIY SOS: The Big Build
programme.desc: The team head to Manchester to help renovate the damp and unsafe home of Rebecca and Steve and their triplets.

programme.title: DIY SOS: The Big Build
programme.desc: Four years ago June Finlay suffered a terrible brain infection which has left her unable to walk, talk and totally dependent on her devoted husband John. Nick and the DIY SOS team extend the house and give June back her dignity.

programme.title: 999: Rescue Squad
programme.desc: A major fire breaks out in a haulage depot in Brighouse and a serious accident at an illegal off-road biking site leads to a rider needing urgent hospital treatment.

programme.title: Psychopath with Piers Morgan
programme.desc: In this groundbreaking and new documentary format, Piers Morgan ventures behind bars for an encounter with diagnosed psychopath Paris Bennett, a man who is more intelligent, more cunning and more manipulative than any criminal Piers has met before.

programme.title: Stacey Dooley Sleeps Over
programme.desc: Stacey spends the weekend with former glamour model Carla Bellucci, who built her career around creating controversial headlines about herself and her family.

programme.title: Prison Girls
programme.desc: Heroin addict Missy is visited by her sugar daddy, who gives her the cash she needs to fund her habit. The secret romance between inmates Franklin and Leslie continues to blossom.

programme.title: Prison Girls
programme.desc: Phlebotomist Julia has been charged with the murder of her ex-boyfriend. Now, she has to learn to navigate jail for the first time. Inmate Rachel fails a drug test, and the full weight of prison rule comes down on her.

programme.title: Louis Theroux Under the Knife
programme.desc: Louis Theroux pulls back the folds of plastic America and gets under the skin of the plastic surgery patients that crowd the clinics of Beverly Hills. Louis becomes a part of the plastic surgeons daily lives, joining them for their consultations and operations in the search for everlasting youth. As Louis tries to understand these motivations, he wonders if he too might be a candidate for a nip tuck.

programme.title: Emma Willis: Delivering Babies
programme.desc: Emma wants to create a good first impression, but her first day doesnt start as planned when she forgets to bring the right shoes and has to call her husband to come to her rescue.

programme.title: Teleshopping
programme.desc: Shopping from home.

programme.title: Gemma and Gorka: Life Behind the Lens
programme.desc: A half-term holiday to Madrid gives the family a break, a chance to soak up some Spanish culture, and a long-awaited first meeting between Thiago and his grandparents!

programme.title: Find It, Fix It, Flog It
programme.desc: Henry Cole and Simon OBrien are in the Cotswolds and Somerset, where their finds include a set of milk churns and an old munitions shell.

programme.title: DIY SOS: The Big Build
programme.desc: Nick Knowles and the team renovate the house of Colin and Clare Gibbons and adapt it to meet the needs of their eldest son Christopher, who suffers from Duchenes muscular dystrophy.

programme.title: DIY SOS: The Big Build
programme.desc: Nick and the team head to Warrington help adapt the home of Dave McCartney, who suffered a massive stroke and is now forced to sleep in a hospital bed in the kitchen.

programme.title: My Dream Home
programme.desc: Couple Julie and Ryan moved back to Nashville to be closer to family. However, they are having trouble finding the perfect home of their own. The Scott twins help in the search for a kid-friendly house.

programme.title: My Dream Home
programme.desc: A musical couple, Ashley and Aben, is eager to find their forever home near Nashvilles legendary Music Row. They seek the help of twins Drew and Jonathan to find their dream home.

programme.title: DIY SOS: The Big Build
programme.desc: Nick Knowles and the team call on the local community to renovate the home of Rob and Michelle Wall and their son Noah, who suffers from spina bifida.

programme.title: DIY SOS: The Big Build
programme.desc: Nick Knowles and the team renovate the house of Colin and Clare Gibbons and adapt it to meet the needs of their eldest son Christopher, who suffers from Duchenes muscular dystrophy.

programme.title: Pete Wicks: For Dogs Sake
programme.desc: Pete meets the four-legged friends that call the UKs leading dog welfare charity, Dogs Trust, home. From when they are rescued, Pete plays an instrumental role in their rehabilitation process, right up until the point the dogs are safely rehomed.

programme.title: Louis Theroux: The Most Hated Family in America
programme.desc: Louis struggles to come to terms with the infamous Phelps family from Topeka, Kansas who picket the funerals of dead soldiers killed in Iraq as a protest against an America that tolerates homosexuality. The family members, some as young as six years old, wave slogans with messages such as `God Hates Fags, Thank God for 9/11, and God Hates You at pickets all over the States.

programme.title: Louis Theroux: Americas Most Hated Family in Crisis
programme.desc: Following up on his 2007 documentary, `The Most Hated Family in America, Louis Theroux returns to Topeka, Kansas, for a week-long visit with the Westboro Baptist Church. He again joins the Phelps family on their controversial pickets where they antagonise communities with offensive slogans. However, four years on from Louiss last visit, there are signs of disarray in the Phelps clan.

programme.title: Stacey Dooley
programme.desc: Stacey travels to America to meet people prepping for an uncertain future, all with different concerns - from climate change and natural disasters to civil unrest.

programme.title: Prison Girls
programme.desc: In the jails intake area, women fresh off the streets can be drunk, high or mentally unwell. With inmates detoxing from heroin, crack, and crystal meth, medical emergencies are a regular occurrence.

programme.title: Emma Willis: Delivering Babies
programme.desc: In her second week at Watford General Hospital, Emma must step up when shes asked to take an active role in theatre during a caesarean birth.

programme.title: Emma Willis: Delivering Babies
programme.desc: Nearly halfway through her time at the hospital, Emma gets thrown in at the deep end with birthing pool evacuation training and learning how to remove a catheter.

programme.title: Teleshopping
programme.desc: Shopping from home.

programme.title: Great British Railway Journeys
programme.desc: In Blackburn Michael catches a rare glimpse of Edwardian life on celluloid and marvels at how factory workers and schoolchildren alike were drawn to seek fame on film. Continuing east to Nelson, Michael braves the enemy camp to have a pint of tea with the socialist working classes in Britains last Clarion House. Way out of his comfort zone, he is heartened by their warm welcome.

programme.title: Great British Railway Journeys
programme.desc: In Manchester at the magnificent Heaton Park, Michael discovers one of the first open air concerts was held here in 1909 by an opera fan, William Grimshaw, who entertained 40,000 people to the music of opera superstar, Enrico Caruso, on a gramophone.

programme.title: Great British Railway Journeys
programme.desc: Michael heads for Maltby, where he is caught up with the thrill of the chase as he investigates the high-octane sport of whippet racing. He learns how this `poor mans sport grew during the 19th century in the mining towns of the north and retains its appeal today.

programme.title: Great British Railway Journeys
programme.desc: Michael Portillo reaches Birmingham and discovers how a radical reformer would make his mark on the city of a thousand trades. At Highbury Hall he finds Joseph Chamberlains splendid mansion home and hears how he established a political dynasty including a Prime Minister and a Foreign Secretary.

programme.title: Bangers and Cash
programme.desc: Derek is on the road to pick up a funky little Nissan Figaro and a sublime Ferrari 250 GT tribute car, and Paul gets to try out his spanking new Scania T Cab.

programme.title: Bangers and Cash
programme.desc: Derek checks out an abandoned 1985 Renault 4 and an MGB Roadster that hasnt seen the light of day for 36 years, and Jack heads to Rotherham to pick up a pair of rare Bond Bugs.

programme.title: Secrets and Spies: A Nuclear Game
programme.desc: With Gordievskys help, Britain identifies Mikhail Gorbachev as a possible new contender for Soviet leader. He could be the ticket to better relations between East and West. However, fragile steps towards diplomacy are threatened by vested interests.

programme.title: Antiques Roadshow
programme.desc: The team are at Woodhorn Museum in Northumberland, a former colliery, where treasures include a gold Rolex from the 1980s and FA Cup medals won by a Newcastle United legend.

programme.title: Antiques Roadshow
programme.desc: Antiques Roadshow returns to Ham House in Richmond, where jewellery specialist John Benjamin is amazed to see a necklace and bracelet found in a bag of compost bought at a garden centre in Germany. Mark Hill is intrigued by a chessboard artwork designed by film-maker Derek Jarman, decorated with pebbles and salvaged objects, while Hilary Kay reveals the secrets of a carved walnut shell that contains a miniature globe.

programme.title: Bangers and Cash: Restoring Classics
programme.desc: Derek encourages the team to buy a Francis Barnett Plover at auction. Plus, the wheels come off the restoration of an MG Metro.

programme.title: Bangers and Cash: Restoring Classics
programme.desc: Derek thinks a 29,000-mile Ford Capri should be kept original. Plus, a Hercules womens racing bike from the 50s.

programme.title: Secrets and Spies: A Nuclear Game
programme.desc: With Gordievskys help, Britain identifies Mikhail Gorbachev as a possible new contender for Soviet leader. He could be the ticket to better relations between East and West. However, fragile steps towards diplomacy are threatened by vested interests.

programme.title: Antiques Roadshow
programme.desc: The team are at Woodhorn Museum in Northumberland, a former colliery, where treasures include a gold Rolex from the 1980s and FA Cup medals won by a Newcastle United legend.

programme.title: Wilderness with Simon Reeve
programme.desc: Simon Reeve travels across the Kalahari in Southern Africa. Along the way, Simon tracks wildebeest with a group of one of the regions indigenous inhabitants, the San, who are renowned for their hunting and tracking techniques.

programme.title: Train Truckers
programme.desc: A cherished wartime iron workhorse needs to be hauled 160 miles from the Churnet Valley Railway in Staffordshire to a winter extravaganza in Oxfordshire.

programme.title: David Jasons Great British Inventions
programme.desc: Sir David Jason finds out about the Penny Farthing as he looks at the British inventors who played a vital part in the evolution of the wheel.

programme.title: Bangers and Cash: Restoring Classics
programme.desc: Derek encourages the team to buy a Francis Barnett Plover at auction. Plus, the wheels come off the restoration of an MG Metro.

programme.title: Bangers and Cash: Restoring Classics
programme.desc: Derek thinks a 29,000-mile Ford Capri should be kept original. Plus, a Hercules womens racing bike from the 50s.

programme.title: Retro Electro Workshop
programme.desc: Shamil buys a bargain Boombox fresh out of the 80s. It looks amazing but Rob predicts it will be a nightmare to repair.

programme.title: Retro Electro Workshop
programme.desc: Shamil picks up an iconic Weltron Space Helmet Radio in need of repair, but the spherical design proves a challenge to even get open.

programme.title: Masters of Reinvention
programme.sub-title: Air Raid Shelter and Pedal-Powered Washing Machine
programme.desc: The team reinvent a vintage DIY air raid shelter as a hi-tech garden shed. Plus, Jenny and Ellis work on a pedal-powered washing machine.

programme.title: Teleshopping
programme.desc: Shopping from home.

programme.title: Great British Railway Journeys
programme.desc: Early 20th century Britain was reeling from industrial strife and suffragette outrages but the biggest crisis of all was the conflict in Ireland. Beginning in Newry, Michael finds a specially chartered train would deliver demonstrators campaigning for Irish Home Rule to a rally in the town.

programme.title: Great British Railway Journeys
programme.desc: In Belfasts grand Edwardian City Hall, Michael investigates the scene of a watershed moment in Irish history and hears how thousands of Ulster Protestants were mobilised by train to sign a document pledging opposition to Home Rule for Ireland.

programme.title: Great British Railway Journeys
programme.desc: Leaving from the seaport of Larne Michael reaches Stranraerand the Mull of Galloway, where in a lighthouse built by railway engineer, Robert Stevenson, he discovers a magnificent machine, installed at the turn of the 20th century.

programme.title: Great British Railway Journeys
programme.desc: Michael Portillo continues his journey through western Scotland by exploring the industrial heartland of Glasgow and its mighty River Clyde before taking the ferry to the island of Cumbrae. He is put to work behind the scenes at Glasgows circular subway, explores the future of shipbuilding on the Clyde.

programme.title: Bangers and Cash
programme.desc: Derek heads to Northumberland to collect a rare 1975 NSU RO 80, Jack draws the short straw again with a Mini Mayfair stuck in a barn, and an Aston Martin DBS husk is auctioned.

programme.title: Bangers and Cash
programme.desc: On the way to pick up two lovely 70s Lotus sports cars, Derek takes to the skies for his first ever helicopter flight in a 1967 Bell 47 chopper.

programme.title: Secrets and Spies: A Nuclear Game
programme.desc: Its 1985, and Mikhail Gorbachev is the new, young leader of the Soviet Union at a very tense time. While both sides have hopes of peace, they have been publicly stockpiling nuclear weapons for decades, and neither wants to appear weak.

programme.title: Antiques Roadshow
programme.desc: The Roadshow visits the Bishops Palace, a site of special religious significance for over 800 years in the heart of Wells in Somerset. On a hot summers day, Marc Allum reveals that a modest Delft plaque from a Bristol pub, decorated with a drinking song by composer Henry Purcell, could be of national importance, with a value to match.

programme.title: Antiques Roadshow
programme.desc: Fiona and the team return to the beautiful grounds of the Royal Botanic Garden Edinburgh, where finds include a stunning set of traditional Highland dress that once belonged to a clan chieftain and a rare Rolex Sea-Dweller watch. Siobhan Tyrell is thrilled to see a selection of medals awarded to pioneering diver Verrall Newman, including a 1924 Olympic participants medal, while Susan Rumfitt hears the poignant story behind a Norwegian bridal brooch.

programme.title: Bangers and Cash: Restoring Classics
programme.desc: Derek picks out a solid-looking 1969 MGB for the team to look out for at auction. After winning it is sent to a father-and-son team to restore.

programme.title: Bangers and Cash: Restoring Classics
programme.desc: Dave alerts the team to a 1979 Lotus Esprit S3. The car is a thoroughbred classic that desperately needs a proper restoration, but its also an expensive purchase that risks not paying off.

programme.title: Secrets and Spies: A Nuclear Game
programme.desc: Its 1985, and Mikhail Gorbachev is the new, young leader of the Soviet Union at a very tense time. While both sides have hopes of peace, they have been publicly stockpiling nuclear weapons for decades, and neither wants to appear weak.

programme.title: Antiques Roadshow

On a hot summers day, Marc Allum reveals that a modest Delft plaque from a Bristol pub, decorated with a drinking song by composer Henry Purcell, could be of national importance, with a value to match.

programme.title: Operation Arctic with Simon Reeve
programme.desc: Simon travels to an unspoilt corner of Greenland that could become one of the biggest uranium mines in the world, while Kate follows the oil money thats shaped Alaska. In Churchill, Gordon learns how to stay safe when living side by side with the worlds largest living land carnivore.

programme.title: Bangers and Cash
programme.desc: Jack has his eye on a Peugeot 205 GTI that could be one of the finest examples in the country. Plus, Derek collects a pair of sporty 60s Triumphs.

programme.title: Hunting Outback Gold
programme.desc: In the search for billions of dollars in gold, historian Tanya uncovers a shocking new theory that flips the hunt to the east.

programme.title: Bangers and Cash: Restoring Classics
programme.desc: Derek picks out a solid-looking 1969 MGB for the team to look out for at auction. After winning it is sent to a father-and-son team to restore.

programme.desc: Dave alerts the team to a 1979 Lotus Esprit S3. The car is a thoroughbred classic that desperately needs a proper restoration, but its also an expensive purchase that risks not paying off.

programme.title: Retro Electro Workshop
programme.desc: Shamil finds a pinball machine that should be simple to repair, but just when Rob thinks hes got the flippers flipping, another tricky obstacle gets in the way.

programme.title: Retro Electro Workshop
programme.desc: Shamil relives his childhood memories with a classic Scalextric, but its a new challenge for Rob who never owned one as a child and has never fixed one either.

programme.title: Masters of Reinvention
programme.desc: While updating a vintage aluminium boat, the team disagree over what size and shape it needs to be. Plus, Ivan and Louiza reinvent a cycle sidecar.
